item.title: MUSIC | A journey to the world stage
item.description: LONG before international chart placements, radio airplay and millions of streams, Tahi Johnson Fiu was a little boy with a guitar placed gently into his hands. Encouraged by his family, he was introduced to the instrument long before he imagined a career in the lucrative yet cutthroat music industry.
item.content:encoded:
LONG before international chart placements, radio airplay and millions of streams, Tahi Johnson Fiu was a little boy with a guitar placed gently into his hands.

Encouraged by his family, he was introduced to the instrument long before he imagined a career in the lucrative yet cutthroat music industry.

His first guitar became more than a childhood gift. It was a tool that ignited a lifelong passion that continues to grow.

As the years passed, he expanded his musicianship to the bass, piano and other instruments, driven by an endless curiosity not only to perform music but to understand how it was written, arranged and produced.

Growing up as a proud Rotuman-Fijian living in Australia, Fiu learnt that talent alone would never be adequate. Discipline, humility and respect were just as important as technical ability.

Countless hours were spent practising, listening and refining his craft, laying the foundation for a journey that would eventually carry him well beyond Pacific shores.

That commitment was rewarded when he earned admission to the prestigious Berklee College of Music in Boston, Massachusetts.

Today, his time at Berklee stands as the launching pad for an international career built not under the spotlight, but behind the scenes where producers, composers and session musicians quietly shape the music heard around the world.

Since graduating, Fiu has steadily established himself as a respected producer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist and bassist, working with artists across the United States, Europe, Asia and Australia.

His productions have reached audiences well beyond Fiji, with songs he produced accumulating more than 1.9 million Spotify streams across three titles alone.

The artists he has collaborated with collectively boast catalogues that have attracted hundreds of millions of streams worldwide.

Among his most recognised credits is Favela by Dublin rapper KhakiKid, where Fiu laid down the song’s signature bass and guitar parts.

The single climbed to No.10 on the Irish Homegrown Chart compiled by the Official Charts Company and remained in the Top 20 for five consecutive weeks.

It also earned airplay on Ireland’s leading broadcasters, including iRadio, Spin 1038 and the national broadcaster RTÉ 2fm, introducing Fiu’s musicianship to listeners across the country.

His growing reputation continued in the United States through his work on Sorry 2 Disappoint You, the album by Grammy-nominated songwriter Ré Lxuise.

Serving as composer, producer, writer and multi-instrumentalist, Fiu helped deliver a project that reached No.24 on both the Songstats United States and Global R&B/Soul Album charts, while also charting in Finland, Qatar and Azerbaijan.

Tracks from the album received rotation on radio stations in the United States, London and Finland, further expanding his international footprint.

In October 2023, Fiu also provided assistant production for Ré Lxuise’s opening performance for Macy Gray and California Jet Club at Atlanta’s renowned Eastern Theatre.

His portfolio continues to grow through collaborations with internationally recognised artists.

His production and instrumental work with kidsnot$aints has connected him to an artist whose catalogue has surpassed 42 million Spotify streams.

His bass and guitar performances feature on Donny Daydream’s VACANCY EP from Hong Kong, while he has also recorded with acclaimed Korean guitarist Rock Choi, an endorsed artist for D’Angelico Guitars, Jim Dunlop and Electro-Harmonix.Live performance has remained equally important throughout his journey.

Together with fellow musician Timothee Alex, Fiu formed the duo T&T, performing at Berklee’s inaugural Grad Fest at the House of Blues in Boston in 2023.

He has also shared stages internationally, including performances supporting Australian artist KIMMI at Sydney’s Oxford Art Factory.

What is unusual about Fiu’s story, viewed from Suva, is not that a musician with Rotuman roots has reached the international stage—Fiji has produced globally recognised artists for generations. Rather, it is that he has built his career from the production chair.

Bass players, producers and session musicians are rarely the names printed in the biggest headlines. More often, they are the creative force whose work appears in the credits rather than on the marquee.

The recognition accumulating around Fiu’s name has come from chart compilers, broadcasters and editorial curators within the music industry itself, reflecting the respect he has earned among professionals.

For young Fijian and Rotuman musicians who dream not only of performing but also of writing, arranging and producing music, Fiu’s career offers a tangible example of what is possible.

His growing catalogue demonstrates that success in the modern music industry extends well beyond the stage and into the recording studio, where songs are imagined, shaped and brought to life.

Now based in Los Angeles, Fiu continues to collaborate with artists, producers and songwriters from around the world, including Sony songwriter and producer Raphael George, while expanding a career that continues to connect Fiji to the international music industry.

Despite the growing list of achievements, chart successes and global collaborations, Fiu remains grounded in the values that first inspired his journey.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | The backbone to your fleet that’s tough and proven
item.description: THE Hino 500 Series has the power and performance you need. Nimble and streamlined, the Hino 500 can easily move carefully in tight spaces, making it ideal to meet the transport needs of the logistics, construction and manufacturing industries. It is also an excellent transport solution for general freight, including refrigerated cargo. Hino protects your business by protecting your driver.
item.content:encoded:
THE Hino 500 Series has the power and performance you need. Nimble and streamlined, the Hino 500 can easily move carefully in tight spaces, making it ideal to meet the transport needs of the logistics, construction and manufacturing industries.

It is also an excellent transport solution for general freight, including refrigerated cargo. Hino protects your business by protecting your driver.

Hino considers safety from every angle, from an anti-fatigue pedal and switch layout in the cab, to reinforced main frames and door beams, to a steering column that collapses in the event of a collision.

Maximizing driver safety is one way that Hino maximizes its commitment to you as a business partner.

The newest model in the range has a full floating cab, full dual air suspension on the rear, as well as

ABS. Side impact beams on both the driver and passenger doors help prevent injury in the event of an accident. It also has a new Euro 4 common rail engine, reducing unwanted CO2 with added driver convenience and comfort, radio and air conditioning are standard.

The Hino frame provides unbeatable reliability and even greater durability with improved rust resistance. While the frame has been made lighter to improve fuel efficiency, it still provides greater load capacity.

A larger air intake duct means better fuel efficiency and the pre-cleaning air intake system improves operating costs.

Entering/Exiting made easy with optimal step-to-floor distance and an enlarged handgrip.

Daily maintenance checks are made simple with the easily accessible front panel.

It has what it takes to get the job done right, the first time, every time.

The Hino 500 Series is up for any challenge.

When it comes to driver comfort, the interior was designed to provide the best in operability with a low profile and uncluttered belt line to secure excellent frontal vision and a functional instrument panel that is designed to be easy to see and easy to use, further reducing driver fatigue.

A wide selection of Hino trucks available, you will be well geared in driving the success of your business.

Hino Series are available at Niranjan’s Hino Dealership in Suva, Nadi, Lautoka and Labasa Source: Niranjan Autoport Pte Ltd.

Discover the road ahead with Hino Trucks.

Hino considers safety from every angle, from an anti-fatigue pedal and switch layout in the cab, to reinforced main frames and door beams, to a steering column that collapses in the event of a collision. Pictures: Niranjans

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Minivan owner faces tough times
item.description: FOR 48-year-old Ravinesh Pal, every day begins long before sunrise. As the owner of three minivans operating between Suva and Nausori, he understands the challenges of the transport industry better than most.
item.content:encoded:
FOR 48-year-old Ravinesh Pal, every day begins long before sunrise.

As the owner of three minivans operating between Suva and Nausori, he understands the challenges of the transport industry better than most.

Despite the rising cost of fuel and increasing financial pressures, he continues to work tirelessly to support his family and provide reliable service to his passengers.

Originally from Koronivia, Nausori, Ravinesh has spent the last 25 years behind the wheel.

Today, two of his minivans are driven by hired drivers, while he personally operates the third.

His commitment to the business reflects years of hard work, determination, and passion.

Speaking about his journey, Ravinesh recalled his early career before entering the transport industry.

“Before becoming a minivan operator, I worked at BP Oil. I was a manager there and worked for five years. “After that, I decided to start my own business. It was a big step, but I believed in myself and worked hard to build what I have today,” he said.

His daily routine is demanding and requires long hours on the road.

“I start my day at 4:30 in the morning from Nausori. It is a hustle every day.

“I keep working until around 6pm before heading home. It’s not easy, but it is how I provide for my family,” he shared.

Ravinesh lives with his wife and two children, who remain his greatest motivation.

Despite the long hours, he finds joy in serving the people who rely on his service each day.

“I have regular passengers who know me and my van. I always greet them with a smile and make time to chat with them. After a long day, those conversations make the work worthwhile,” he said.

Driving has always been more than just a job for Ravinesh it has been a lifelong dream. “Growing up, I used to watch drivers on the road and admire what they did. I would tell myself that one day I would become a driver. Today, that dream has come true, and I’m grateful for that.”

While reflecting on the changes he has witnessed over the years, Ravinesh expressed concern about some of the challenges facing today’s younger generation.

“A lot is happening in society now that we didn’t face during our youth. There are issues like drugs, alcohol abuse, robbery, and other problems.

“Young people need to make the right choices in life. If they change their mindset and focus on positive things, they can build a better future for themselves and for Fiji.

Like many transport operators across the country, Ravinesh says the rising cost of fuel has become one of the biggest challenges affecting the industry.

“We all know fuel prices are very high now. Back in the day, we could make a decent income and comfortably provide for our families.

“Today, with fuel prices increasing, many of us are losing hope because it is becoming harder to make ends meet,” he said.

He acknowledged that every business experiences ups and downs but believes current conditions are among the toughest he has faced.

“In business, there will always be challenges, but this period has been especially difficult. We can only hope that things improve soon.”

Ravinesh ended with a heartfelt message for Fiji’s youth.

“Focus on your education and work hard for your families. Young people are the future of this country.

“If we choose the wrong path today, the future will be difficult for everyone. Stay positive, make good decisions, and together we can make Fiji a better place.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: PROPERTY GUIDE | From bare to bold
item.description: Do the walls in your living room or bedroom feel flat and boring? Try these ten ideas for textured walls that’ll transform your space from blah to wow-worthy in a weekend. Make a statement in your powder room, living room, dining room or bedroom with wallpaper. Grasscloth wallpaper brings warmth and texture. Try textured wallpaper that’s paintable to match your room’s decor. There’s also wallpaper
item.content:encoded:
Do the walls in your living room or bedroom feel flat and boring? Try these ten ideas for textured walls that’ll transform your space from blah to wow-worthy in a weekend.

Make a statement in your powder room, living room, dining room or bedroom with wallpaper. Grasscloth wallpaper brings warmth and texture. Try textured wallpaper that’s paintable to match your room’s decor. There’s also wallpaper that mimics different wall textures such as wood plank, brick, marble, terrazzo and more. Peel-and-stick wallpaper is great for renters with the ability to easily remove it without damaging your walls when you move out.

Decorative moulding is one of the easiest and most affordable ways to turn plain white walls into bold and sophisticated spaces. Choose moulding for any style including: art deco, Victorian, craftsman, mid-century modern styles and much more.

If you prefer a more traditional or European feel, picture moulding is a great option. If you love a modern farmhouse or coastal aesthetic, board and batten is an appropriate choice. Are you ready to get started?

Plastered cement walls have made a comeback in the last few years, but real plastered walls are expensive to create. We have four faux treatments that will add warmth and depth to your space. Mimic the look with wallpaper, joint compound wall treatment, Venetian plaster paint or limewash paint.

The kitchen that you see above was created with a limewash paint that’s made from natural lime, limestone, powdered marble, pigments and eco-friendly binders. It can be brushed, rolled, sprayed or sponged to walls, ceilings, plaster, brick, stone and more. Choose from a textured or smooth finish.

Beadboard is usually found in bathrooms and kitchens in more traditional or rustic settings. It’s typically installed on the bottom half of walls with a chair rail on top. But it can also be used in mudrooms, laundry rooms, hallways and even on the ceiling.

Installation is quick and easy. Beadboard is usually found in white, but it can be painted to match your wall color.

As the modern farmhouse trend rose in popularity, shiplap became an interior design staple. But it’s actually been around for hundreds of years. Originally used in shipbuilding, it was often used on the exterior of homes, barns and sheds.

Shiplap works well in coastal homes, rustic settings and traditional homes. As shown, it brings texture and depth to a bright entryway. But it can be used in mudrooms, laundry rooms, powder rooms and as accent walls in a living or dining space.

Fluted walls can be found everywhere these days. These panels can be painted, stained or found in warm wood tones that work well in modern spaces. Installation is simple by using paneling adhesive or a brad nailer.

This is an easy DIY project that can be done in a weekend to add texture to your space. Install on kitchen islands, banquettes, cabinet doors, walls and more.

Originally used in shipbuilding, shiplap walls were often used on the exterior of homes, barns and sheds. Picture:WWW.LOWES.COM

Fluted walls can be painted, stained or found in warm wood tones that work well in modern spaces. Picture:WWW.LOWES.COM

Decorative moulding is one of the easiest and most affordable ways to turn plain white walls into bold and sophisticated spaces. Picture: WWW.LOWES.COM

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | 2026 SUBARU CROSSTREK 2.0L SUV
item.description: The new 2026 Subaru Crosstrek has been a trusted partner for customers interested in drivability, utility, safety and those who enjoy nature and are involved in outdoor sports and activities. Subaru Crosstrek features have been created to cater for the present and future needs of customers from design, craftsmanship, quality, to engineering and technology, the New Subaru SUV is truly the best Cros
item.content:encoded:
The new 2026 Subaru Crosstrek has been a trusted partner for customers interested in drivability, utility, safety and those who enjoy nature and are involved in outdoor sports and activities.

Subaru Crosstrek features have been created to cater for the present and future needs of customers from design, craftsmanship, quality, to engineering and technology, the New Subaru SUV is truly the best Crosstrek has ever built.

The New Subaru Crosstrek is a subcompact crossover which offers style, versatility, specs, robustness, rugged, fuel efficiency, engineering & most of all – Affordability.

In short, the New 2026 Crosstrek is a package designed to go slightly farther off the pavement than its previous model – The Subaru XV.

The Subaru Crosstrek is available in 2 models: Base Spec & Mid Spec which is equipped with the Subaru Eyesight Driver Assist Technology.

Both, the base Spec and the mid Spec get a 2.0-liter four cylinder Horizontally Opposed (Boxer) – Euro 6 Engine which punches up to 115kw of power.

This engine is the carryover from its Ex-model – providing an excellent fuel economy of 9.7Litre per 100km on Urban and 5.9Litre per 100km on Highway.

Also carrying over from the previous model is the CVT – Continuous Variable Transmission – providing the Crosstrek the ability to deliver the right gear change for optimum performance for daily driving.

The Crosstrek has an improved AWD system which offers quicker response to changing driving conditions and road surface.

Hill start assist is standard, along with Subaru’s X-Mode traction and drive mode technology, which includes hill descent control.

Subaru offers two different approaches to the Crosstrek’s infotainment system.

The base model features a split dual 7-inch touchscreen displays while the Mid Spec, boasts a sharp-looking and responsive 11.6-inch touchscreen display mounted in portrait orientation that dominates the center of the dashboard. The 2026 Crosstrek in mid spec has been upgraded with extra features such as: day time running lights, Led Fog Lamps, a full 11.6″ touch screen and the Subaru Eyesight Driver Assist Technology which offers – Pre-Collision Braking System, Adaptive Cruise Control, Lane Departure Warning, Lead Vehicle Star Alert and Reverse Sensors with Reverse Automatic Braking The main focus for the 2026 Crosstrek is to “bring joy and smiles to our adventure-seeking customers” for every day of the week including vacations.

That means comfort not just in the daily gridlock but on long road trips as well.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Kitchen appliances you can rely on
item.description: THE best moments at home often begin in the kitchen. It is where breakfast starts busy mornings, where favourite family recipes are shared, and where celebrations come alive around the dining table. Every memorable meal deserves appliances you can rely on. That is why Narhari proudly brings you the complete Baumatic and Fiesta cooking range collection, combining quality, performance, and style to
item.content:encoded:
THE best moments at home often begin in the kitchen. It is where breakfast starts busy mornings, where favourite family recipes are shared, and where celebrations come alive around the dining table.

Every memorable meal deserves appliances you can rely on. That is why Narhari proudly brings you the complete Baumatic and Fiesta cooking range collection, combining quality, performance, and style to suit every home and every cooking style.

Baumatic is designed for those who love premium cooking experiences. Its gas ranges deliver outstanding heat control, generous oven capacity, and modern European styling that adds elegance to any kitchen.

Whether you are roasting, baking, grilling, or preparing several dishes at once, Baumatic cooking ranges help you cook with confidence while achieving delicious results every time.

Cooking becomes even more enjoyable with Baumatic gas and ceramic hobs. Fast heating, responsive temperature control, and durable construction make everyday meal preparation smooth and efficient.

Their contemporary appearance blends beautifully with modern kitchen designs while providing the performance serious home cooks appreciate.

Complete the look with a Baumatic rangehood that keeps your kitchen fresh and comfortable.

Powerful extraction helps remove smoke, steam, and cooking odours, allowing you to enjoy a cleaner cooking environment every day.

Pair it with a Baumatic electric skillet to prepare everything from hearty breakfasts to family-sized meals with consistent cooking performance.

Outdoor entertaining also becomes effortless with Baumatic barbecue and planchas, giving you the freedom to grill delicious meals for family gatherings and weekend get-togethers. Finish your kitchen with Baumatic cookware that delivers reliable performance and even heat distribution, making every recipe easier to prepare.

Fiesta cooking ranges are built for busy households that value practicality without compromising quality.

Spacious ovens, dependable gas burners, and easy-to-use controls make daily cooking simple whether you are preparing a quick breakfast, school lunches, or a Sunday feast for the entire family.

Fiesta offers dependable performance that makes every meal easier to prepare.

The Fiesta gas and ceramic hob collection gives you the flexibility to cook several dishes simultaneously while maintaining excellent heat control.

Combined with Fiesta rangehoods, your kitchen stays cleaner and fresher throughout every cooking session, creating a more enjoyable place to spend time with family.

Every kitchen deserves appliances that work as hard as you do. Baumatic delivers premium performance with refined European styling, while Fiesta provides dependable everyday value that families can trust.

Together, they offer a complete cooking solution designed to inspire confidence every time you step into the kitchen.

Now is the perfect opportunity to upgrade your cooking experience.

Discover the complete range of Baumatic and Fiesta cooking appliances at Narhari and bring home quality that will serve your family for years to come.

Suva • Lautoka • Nadi • Ba • Yadua-Sigatoka • Rakiraki

• Nausori • Navua • Savusavu • Nakasi • Labasa.

🌐 Visit our website: www.narharifiji.com

Follow us for the latest deals, promos and product updates:

Facebook: www.facebook.com/narhari.fiji;

Instagram: www.instagram.com/narhari.fj;

TikTok: www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount;

Narhari – TikTok: www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount

Narhari – Quality solutions for your every room.

Right: Upgrade your kitchen today with Baumatic kitchen appliances.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Wash you fruits and vegies just before you eat it
item.description: FRESH fruits and vegetables are an essential part of a healthy diet, providing the vitamins, minerals and nutrients our bodies need every day. Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them washing them properly.
item.content:encoded:
FRESH fruits and vegetables are an essential part of a healthy diet, providing the vitamins, minerals and nutrients our bodies need every day.

Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them washing them properly.

According to allrecipes.com, many people assume that a quick rinse is enough, while others rely on soaps or special fruit washes.

The good news is that cleaning your produce doesn’t have to be complicated. With a few simple techniques, you can remove dirt, reduce residues and enjoy your fresh food with confidence.

Before reaching your kitchen, fruits and vegetables travel a long journey.

They are grown in fields or orchards, handled during harvesting, transported to packing facilities, displayed in stores and often touched by many hands before ending up in your shopping basket.

During that journey, produce can collect dust, soil, bacteria and tiny traces of pesticides. Some fruits and vegetables are also coated with a thin layer of edible wax to help maintain freshness and protect them during transport.

Although these residues are generally considered safe within regulated limits, washing your produce helps remove surface dirt and unwanted particles while giving you peace of mind before eating or cooking.

One of the biggest mistakes people make is washing all their produce as soon as they get home from the supermarket.

While it may seem like a great way to save time, excess moisture can actually cause some fruits and vegetables to spoil more quickly.

Leafy greens, fresh herbs and berries are especially delicate. Washing them before storage can encourage mould growth and shorten their shelf life.

Instead, it’s best to wash fruits and vegetables just before you’re ready to use them.

If you prefer preparing snacks ahead of time, make sure the produce is thoroughly dried before returning it to the refrigerator.

For most fruits and vegetables, the simplest method is also the most effective.

Hold the produce under cool running water while gently rubbing the surface with your hands. This removes most dirt, dust and surface residues without the need for soap, detergent or chemical produce washes.

There’s no need to soak fruits and vegetables in bleach or cleaning solutions, as these products are not designed for food and may leave harmful residues behind.

For firm produce such as potatoes, carrots, cucumbers and apples, using a clean vegetable brush while rinsing under water can help remove stubborn dirt.

Certain fruits and vegetables require slightly different cleaning methods to ensure they are free from grit while staying fresh and intact.

Leeks are well known for trapping soil between their tightly packed layers. Trim off the roots and dark green tops, slice the leek, then place the pieces into a bowl of cold water.

Swish them gently with your hands and lift them out, leaving the grit behind at the bottom of the bowl.

Berries, including raspberries and blackberries, are extremely delicate. Place them in a colander and rinse gently under cool running water just before serving. Spread them on paper towels and gently pat dry to avoid bruising.

Leafy greens and fresh herbs should also be rinsed carefully under cool water. If they contain visible dirt or sand, soak them briefly in a bowl of cold water before lifting them out and draining them in a colander. A salad spinner is an excellent way to remove excess water and keep leaves crisp.

Mushrooms require a gentle touch. Quickly rinse them under running water while rubbing away any dirt with your fingers, then pat dry with a clean paper towel. Avoid soaking mushrooms, as they absorb water easily and can become soggy.

Making produce washing part of your daily routine is one of the easiest ways to support good food hygiene. It takes only a few minutes but helps remove dirt, bacteria and other unwanted particles before meals are prepared.

Pair clean produce with proper food handling by washing your hands before cooking, using clean chopping boards and storing fruits and vegetables correctly in the refrigerator.

Fresh fruits and vegetables are among the healthiest foods you can eat.

By giving them a quick but thorough wash before serving, you’ll enjoy every bite knowing your meals are as fresh, clean and safe as possible.

After all, great meals begin with fresh ingredients, and clean produce is the first step towards healthier eating.

Clean fruits and vegetables are more appealing to eat and prepare. Picture: GOOGLE

Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them — wash it properly. Pictures: GOOGLE

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | VERE’S TAKE | Buy more food from the market
item.description: Bula vinaka everyone, buying more fresh food from the market is the wisest choice right now. After the budget discussions, fuel price concerns and rising household costs, perhaps it’s time to appreciate something we’ve always had, our local food. Tavioka, dalo, rourou, ota, bele, kumala, chilies, lemons, fresh fish, uto, bananas and tropical fruits aren’t just part of us, they’re the most affordab
item.content:encoded:
Bula vinaka everyone, buying more fresh food from the market is the wisest choice right now. After the budget discussions, fuel price concerns and rising household costs, perhaps it’s time to appreciate something we’ve always had, our local food.

Tavioka, dalo, rourou, ota, bele, kumala, chilies, lemons, fresh fish, uto, bananas and tropical fruits aren’t just part of us, they’re the most affordable and nutritious foods available. With imported goods becoming more expensive, one way or the other buying locally has never made more sense.

Every imported apple, orange, packet of frozen vegetables or processed snack has travelled thousands of kilometres before reaching supermarket shelves. Those transport costs eventually end up in consumers’ shopping baskets, and the food is not as fresh as what is available locally in our markets and roadside stalls.

Our locally grown food, reaches markets within a day or two of harvest.

Shopping locally can also stretch a family’s budget. A bundle of rourou or bele costs $2 to $3 far less than packaged salad greens that costs somewhere between $7 to $10 or more, while tavioka and dalo remain staple alternatives to imported rice or other processed foods.

Choosing local doesn’t mean sacrificing variety. Fiji’s cuisine reflects the country’s rich cultural heritage, blending Indigenous, Indo-Fijian, Chinese and European influences. From fresh fish and lovo to fish curry and roti, our everyday meals are proof that local ingredients create world-class dishes, more fresh and affordable.

Buying local food supports farmers, fishermen and market vendors who are navigating the same economic challenges as everyone else. Every dollar spent on local produce stays within Fiji’s economy, helping sustain rural livelihoods and strengthen food security.

Consumers can also reduce food waste by buying seasonal produce. Fruits and vegetables that are in season are generally fresher, more abundant and more affordable than imported alternatives. As households continue adjusting to the realities of the new Budget, one simple strategy stands out, fill your shopping bags with Fiji-grown food.

It’s often cheaper, healthier and fresher and every purchase supports our people and communities. In uncertain economic times, perhaps the smartest shopping decision isn’t finding the next big supermarket special.

It’s rediscovering the value that’s already growing in our own backyard.Healthline.com says buying and eating local food offers benefits that extend beyond the dinner table.

It supports healthier eating, strengthens local economies and reduces environmental impact, making it a smart choice for consumers and communities alike.

Fruits, vegetables and other products are harvested only a day or two before reaching the markets, allowing them to ripen naturally and develop better flavour.

Because they travel shorter distances, they spend less time in storage and generally arrive in better condition than imported produce.

Vitamins and antioxidants begin to decline once produce is harvested, particularly during transport and storage. Since locally grown food reaches consumers more quickly, it is more likely to retain its nutritional value.

Shopping at the market and buying directly from farmers also encourages shoppers to try seasonal and unique foods that may not be available in supermarkets.

It provides an opportunity to learn more about our farmers and develops a stronger connection and appreciation with our agriculture sector.

Choosing local food benefits the environment by reducing the need for long-distance transportation, which lowers greenhouse gas emissions and fuel consumption.

Many locally grown products are sold with little packaging, helping reduce plastic waste.

Supporting sustainable local farming helps protect farmland, biodiversity and natural ecosystems that come with economic benefits.

Money spent on local produce stays within the community, supporting farmers, creating jobs for labourers and strengthens businesses.

Buying directly from producers allows shoppers to appreciate the effort involved in growing local food.

Whether through farmers’ markets, roadside stalls or community supported agriculture programs, eating local is an easy way to enjoy fresher, healthier food.

Shop for more fresh food to improve your family’s dietary choices.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | How to cut your grocery bill
